rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HENRY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, February 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Goodyear Tire and Rubber Company¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Goodyear satisfied its burden of production of the issue of whether the ¿Goodyearite¿ sheet material Mr. Henry worked with contained asbestos. Mr. Henry¿s discovery responses are factually devoid, and it is undisputed that Goodyear stopped producing asbestos containing sheet material in 1969 but plaintiff did not work with ¿Goodyearite¿ until 1970. Mr. Henry¿s evidence does not establish a triable issue whether the¿Goodyearite¿ he worked with contained asbestos. There is no triable issue whether Mr. Henry was exposed to asbestos- containing Cranite, Durabla, Wingfoot, or belt material attributable to Goodyear. It is undisputed that Goodyear was one of three suppliers of Cranite, that it stopped producing asbestos-containing Cranite in 1969, and Mr. Henry has no knowledge of who supplied the Cranite he worked with. Mr. Henry cannot recall working with either Durabla or Wingfoot products and his interrogatory responses are factually devoid on these matters. Mr. Henry has no evidence that the Goodyear belt material he worked with contained asbestos. Because the tentative ruling on the exposure issue if confirmed is sufficient to grant Goodyear¿s motion, there is no tentative ruling on the statute of limitations issue. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HENRY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, February 8, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Crane Co¿s joinder in Goodyear¿s motion is denied. The joinder was filed and served less than 75 days before the date of hearing. =(220/HEK)
